name: Read Excel Advanced
description: Advanced Excel Reader Node – Load one or more sheets, named ranges, or specific cell ranges from .xlsx/.xls files with full control over headers, data types, and metadata columns. Perfect for business reports, financial models, and mixed-format Excel sources.
input: Reads data from Excel files (.xlsx, .xls) with support for sheets, ranges, and custom schemas.
output: Returns a DataFrame containing the Excel data with proper column names and types.
hint: Infer the schema when a new file/sheet is selected or the content has changed

fields.name: path
fields.title: Path
fields.description: Full path to the Excel file or folder. Supports local paths, HDFS, S3, GCS, Azure, etc. Example: /data/sales.xlsx or s3a://my-bucket/reports/

fields.name: recdirectorysearch
fields.title: Read subdirectory recursively
fields.description: If the path is a directory, recursively scan all subfolders for Excel files. Useful when processing many files at once.

fields.name: readAllSheets
fields.title: Read All Sheets
fields.description: Automatically read every sheet in the workbook and union them into one DataFrame. Great for files where each sheet has the same structure (e.g., monthly tabs).

fields.name: selectedSheets
fields.title: Select Sheets
fields.description: Comma-separated list of sheet names or indices to import (e.g., Sheet1,Summary,2023 Data or 0,2,5). Leave empty if reading only one sheet or using Read All Sheets.

fields.name: dataAddressMode
fields.title: Data Source Mode
fields.description:
Choose how to specify which part of the sheet to read:
FullSheet Read the entire sheet starting from the defined start line (default & fastest)
CellRange - Import only a specific cell range (e.g., A5:Z1000). Ideal for very large sheets or when you only need a table inside the sheet
NamedRange - Use a predefined named range from Excel (e.g., SalesData, ReportTable). Most reliable when the table position changes

fields.name: startLine
fields.title: Start Data Import on Line
fields.description: Row number (1 = first row) where actual data begins. Useful when Excel files have titles, logos, or blank rows at the top.

fields.name: range
fields.title: Range
fields.description: Excel-style cell range in A1 notation (e.g., A1:F1000, B5:Z500). Limits reading to only these cells - perfect for very large sheets.

fields.name: header_option
fields.title: Header Handling Mode
fields.description:
How to treat headers:
• NO_HEADER - columns become _c0, _c1, etc.
• FIRST_ROW - first row at Start Line becomes column names
• PREVIOUS_ROW - row before Start Line is header
• CUSTOM_ROW - specify exact header row(s)

fields.name: customHeaderRow
fields.title: Custom Header Row
fields.description: Row number or range (e.g., 5 or 5:7 for multi-row headers) to use as header. Only active when Header Handling Mode = CUSTOM_ROW.

fields.name: importSheetNamesOnly
fields.title: Import Only Sheet Names
fields.description: Instead of data, output a simple list of all sheet names in the workbook. Ideal for discovery or dynamic sheet selection workflows.

fields.name: dropSpecialCharacterInColumnName
fields.title: Drop Special Character In Column Name
fields.description: Automatically clean column names by replacing spaces, #, *, /, etc. with underscores and removing invalid characters. Recommended for downstream compatibility.

fields.name: outputFileName
fields.title: Output File Name as Field
fields.description:
Add source file info as a new column:
• no - nothing added
• filename - just the file name (e.g., report.xlsx)
• fullpath - complete path (useful for traceability)

fields.name: sheetNameDisplay
fields.title: Output Sheet Name as Field
fields.description: Adds a new column sheet_name containing the source sheet for each row. Essential when reading multiple sheets together.

fields.name: enforceSchema
fields.title: Enforce Schema
fields.description: Forcefully apply the defined schema (column names & types). Ignores Excel headers completely and casts all data - use when Excel headers are unreliable or missing.

fields.name: outputColNames
fields.title: Column Names for the Excel
fields.description: Explicitly define output column names. Overrides any header from Excel. Leave empty to use Excel headers (or auto-generated names).

fields.name: outputColTypes
fields.title: Column Types for the Excel
fields.description: Force column data types (String, Integer, Long, Double, Boolean, Date, Timestamp). Critical for correct calculations and joins.

fields.name: outputColFormats
fields.title: Column Formats for the Excel
fields.description: Date/timestamp format patterns (e.g., yyyy-MM-dd, dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm:ss). Only needed when Excel stores dates as text or custom formats.

fields.name: limitInputRecords
fields.title: Limit Input Records
fields.description: Maximum rows to preview in interactive/design mode (default 20). Does not affect batch execution.

fields.name: dataSampling
fields.title: Data Sampling
fields.description:
How to sample data in interactive mode:
• Random - picks random rows
• Selective - use a filter expression to choose exactly which rows to preview

fields.name: selectiveSamplingExpression
fields.title: Selective Filter Expression
fields.description: filter condition to pull specific rows in interactive mode (e.g., "Region = 'North' AND Year = 2024"). Only active when Data Sampling = Selective.
